Skokie, Illinois  ·  hodc.org
Serving northern Illinois since 1983
Inside · three-panel spread
2026–2028 Strategic Plan

Where We Stand

HODC builds and manages quality, affordable housing across northern Cook County, with 35 developments completed or underway. Regional need is intensifying just as state funding shifts, making diversified strategy essential.

Mission
We invest in places that people call home. HODC builds and manages quality, affordable housing, creating opportunities for people to not only live, but also thrive.
Vision
We envision a society where access to quality, affordable housing is a reality and housing insecurity is a thing of the past.
The Plan

Strategic Priorities

1
Build Organizational Excellence
Enhance organizational infrastructure to support a high-performing, financially sustainable organization with the capacity to deliver on our mission.
2
Strengthen Community Relationships & Impact
Position HODC as the leading affordable housing developer through deep community relationships and strategic communications.
3
Expand & Diversify Funding
Build sustainable revenue capacity that reduces dependence on any single funding source while supporting organizational growth.
Implementation

Our 3-Year Timeline

Year 1
Financial stability, organizational infrastructure, and community-engagement foundation.
Years 2–3
Revenue diversification, partnership expansion, and capacity scaling.
Progress Since Our Last Plan
  • Broke ground on Zion Woods, Taylor Place, and Poupard Place.
  • Closed our inaugural capital campaign, raising over $0.5M.
  • Diversified our board and clarified governance.
Key Challenge
42% of rental income is federally subsidized — a potential funding freeze threatens both development and operations.
